title: SECRETS.md frontmatter (AIP-19)
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter portion of an AIP-19 SECRETS.md manifest. The manifest declares secret slugs + access policy + audit metadata. Values are NEVER stored in the manifest.

slug.description: Machine identifier. Lowercase, digits, dashes; optional <namespace>/ prefix. 2–80 chars total. Unique within the workspace inventory.

kind.description: Value shape. 'opaque' = single string. 'oauth' = { accessToken, refreshToken, expiresAt }. 'keypair' = { public, private }. 'json' = arbitrary structured.

backend.description: Vault URI (optional). Default: host-resolved by slug. Setting it explicitly exposes infra topology and SHOULD be reviewer-audited.

exposures.description: How this secret is exposed to the runtime that consumes it (env var, config file, egress placeholder, …). Optional hints — hosts MAY honor or remap based on their own catalog. Discriminated by `kind`. New variants slot in without schema breakage; consumers ignore unknown kinds.

allOf.comment: VALUE FIELDS BLOCKED. A SECRETS.md manifest MUST NOT contain plaintext values, ciphertexts, decryption keys, pre-signed URLs, or anything that decrypts.

rotate.description: Reserved for a future rotation AIP. Hosts MAY ignore.

accessEntry.description: A single access-grant entry. Exactly ONE of role/userId/cap/tool/workflow.

exposure.description: A single exposure descriptor — declares HOW the secret value reaches the runtime consumer. Discriminated by `kind`. Hosts that don't recognize a `kind` MUST ignore the entry rather than fail.

name.description: Env var name as the runtime sees it.

field.description: Field on the secret value (after host-side resolution) whose value seeds the env var. Required.

path.description: Absolute path or `~`-relative path inside the runtime sandbox.

field.description: Field whose value seeds the file body. Omit when host-side `wrap` produces synthetic content.

oneOf.title: egress-substitute
oneOf.description: Eligible for substitution at the egress proxy (see @agentproto/egress). Agents see only `$$SECRET[<placeholderName>]$$` placeholders; the proxy materializes the real value at the network boundary.

placeholderName.description: Token agents reference. Convention: matches the env name on the same secret.

allowedByDefault.description: Default value for the per-install allowlist flag. Hosts MAY offer a per-install override.

intendedProviders.description: Audit/UX hint — providers this secret is meant for. NOT enforced at the proxy.

consumerBinding.description: Per-manifest top-level `secrets:` block: env-var name → resolution source. Imported by TOOL.md / WORKFLOW.md / code-workspace manifests via $ref.

propertyNames.description: Env-var names — uppercase letters, digits, underscores; must start with a letter.

consumerBindingEntry.description: Resolution spec for one env-var. Exactly ONE of vault, oauth, value.

vault.description: Slug of an entry in the workspace's SECRETS.md inventory. Resolved via the AIP-19 reveal pipeline (audit + access check).

oauth.description: Provider id of an OAuth connector configured for the workspace (e.g. 'github', 'google'). Returns a fresh access token at reveal time.

value.description: Plain literal value. Not a secret — for non-sensitive config (region codes, feature flags, default locales) declared alongside the real secrets.
